stemHtml: Regarding the Control Wheel Steering (CWS) and Touch Control Steering (TCS) modes of an autopilot. What are the main differences?

options.a: In CWS mode the autopilot servos remain engaged, whereas in TCS the servos are temporarily disengaged while the pilot repositions the controls.
options.b: In CWS mode the servos are disconnected, whereas in TCS they remain engaged.
options.c: CWS is used only during approach, while TCS is used only in cruise.
options.d: There is no functional difference; CWS and TCS are different names for the same mode.
